results.title: Global Supply Chain Finance Intern job with Kraft Heinz Company | 13925390
highlights:
Job DescriptionWhat's on the menuWe have an excellent opportunity for an enthusiastic Bachelor or Master student who is interested in making a difference in the Global Supply Chain Finance team at Kraft Heinz. In Kraft Heinz you'll be the owner of your ideas and of your career. We offer an upbeat and innovative work environment. Besides from your daily tasks, you are motivated to speak up and generate new insights. We appreciate a new point of view! Our team consists of creative problem solvers, and high performers worldwide. We stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we redefine the food industry with daring thinking and outstanding results. If you share our passion - and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen - there's only one thing to do: Join our table and let's make life delicious! Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy and Collaboration We're not afraid to think differently! Adopt new insights. Dream big. We empower our people- from entry-level intern to senior leader - to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners - to be mindful of the collective and sustained success - which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of our team, we want to help you grow and achieve. You are encouraged to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level. You're given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you won't do it alone. You're supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress. The Team Global Supply Chain Finance owns operational performance at Kraft Heinz together with key global and regional stakeholders. The team drives analytics, strategy, and capabilities across the manufacturing and logistics functions to deliver best-in-class cost savings. Summary As a Supply Chain Finance Analyst you will assist in managing finance processes the Global entity overseeing all Zones, providing information and guidance on various financial rituals and routines, monthly performance reviews, financial reviews, and forecasting. You will also support ad-hoc requests and analysis, ensure data availability and accessibility. Additionally, you will be the point of contact for the Capex/P&L management tool. The Role We are looking for an ambitious Supply Chain Finance intern to join the team for a period of 6 months and support various financial reporting, analytics, and strategy projects, including but not limited to:

- Analyzing cost inflation for the current year as well as forward-looking strategy plans and budgets.

- Collaborating with global and regional teams to improve performance rituals and routines.

- Innovating existing processes and driving automation of financial performance analytics.

highlights:
Associate Brand Manager - Innovation (Multiple Positions) - RevOps Careers

# Associate Brand Manager – Innovation (Multiple Positions)

On Site Full Time Chicago, Illinois, United States Kraft Heinz

We are hiring Associate Brand Managers into multiple Innovation Marketing teams. You will play a crucial role in driving the development of new & delicious products from concept to launch. This position requires a creative and analytical thinker with a passion for food innovation and the ability to work collaboratively across various departments.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- Lead the ideation, development and execution of new products (Innovation and/or Renovation projects)
- Analyze market trends, data, and competitive intelligence to identify trends and opportunities
- Manage the product commercialization process and lead cross-functional teams (including R&D, Marketing, Culinary, Consumer Insights, Supply chain, Sales & Finance) ensuring timely, on budget and successful project completion
- Partner with the Marketing & Sales team to develop compelling product launch plans, product positioning, packaging, and promotional strategies and customer & sales presentations

## Benefits

- Coverage for employees (and their eligible dependents) through affordable access to healthcare, protection, and saving for the future
- Wellbeing programs that inspire a physical, social, emotional, and financial well-being lifestyle for our employees and their families

To apply for this job please visit heinz.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com.

results.title: From the Pantry to Game Day: How Kraft Heinz Keeps Iconic Brands at the Center of Culture
highlights:
In this episode of The Speed of Culture podcast, Todd Kaplan, chief marketing officer at Kraft Heinz North America, joins Matt Britton to talk about keeping 70 iconic brands at the center of culture, from the game-day experience to the algorithm-driven future of discovery.
[...]
The conversation covers the generational divide in media consumption, Kraft Heinz’s new five-year partnership with the NFL, what it takes to build an entrepreneurial mindset inside a Fortune 500 company, and how AI is playing a supporting—not starring—role in the brand’s future.
[...]
At Kraft Heinz, he is responsible for driving growth across more than 50 categories by leveraging consumer insights, securing large-scale partnerships like the company’s new global deal with the NFL, and building an internal culture of agility and entrepreneurship. His approach combines analytical rigor with a challenger mindset that treats a Fortune 500 company like a platform for innovation.
[...]
[07:59] AI and the Future of Discoverability — The conversation touches on how Kraft Heinz uses proprietary in-house tools to speed up creative development, versioning, and personalization. Todd frames AI as an efficiency layer—one that frees the marketing team to focus on strategy and creative thinking—rather than a replacement for human judgment. He also notes that brands must remain visible in AI-driven discovery environments, but keeps the focus on agility over automation.

results.title: Algorithmic Gatekeeping Is the New Shelf: What Kraft Heinz's CMO Knows About the AI Discovery Era | Speed of Culture
highlights:
The most important real estate in consumer goods is no longer the end cap at your local grocery store. It is the shortlist an AI model generates when a shopper asks a question out loud. When Todd Kaplan, Chief Marketing Officer of Kraft Heinz North America, sat down with Matt Britton on The Speed of Culture podcast, he framed the stakes in one unforgiving sentence: if someone asks an AI what the best brands are for a task and you are not in that answer, you are invisible.
[...]
That single insight reframes a century of brand building. Kraft Heinz owns 70 brands in North America with 96% household penetration. Some of those brands are 150 years old. And yet the question every CMO must now answer is whether decades of equity will even register when a large language model decides what to recommend. Matt Britton, founder and CEO of Suzy and bestselling author of Generation AI, has spent two decades advising Fortune 500 leaders on consumer evolution. His read on the Kaplan conversation is direct: the discovery layer is being rebuilt, and brands that treat AI visibility as a future project are already losing share they cannot see.
[...]
The data backs the urgency. Roughly 37% of product discovery queries now begin inside AI interfaces like ChatGPT and Perplexity rather than a search bar. Research from GEO firm Brandlight shows the overlap between top Google links and AI-cited sources has collapsed from 70% to below 20%. Ranking on page one no longer guarantees you appear in the answer. The shelf moved, and most brands are still merchandising the old aisle. What follows is a breakdown of the forces Kaplan and Britton mapped on the podcast, and what they demand of any leader who intends to still be discoverable in three years.
[...]
like Kraft Heinz
[...]
household penetration was built in a world where consumers physically encountered products
[...]
, the gatekeeper
[...]
Kaplan put the practical
[...]
The discipline that addresses algorithmic gatekeeping is AEO, or answer engine optimization. AEO is the practice of structuring content and entity data so AI systems can find, understand, and cite a brand inside generated responses. Where SEO optimized for ranked links and clicks, AEO optimizes for citation and inclusion in the answer itself.
[...]
- Audit your share of answer now. Treat AI visibility
[...]
ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity as a measurable KPI, not a curiosity. If you
[...]
are cited, you
[...]
defend the position.
[...]
Answer engine optimization, or AEO, is the practice of structuring content and entity data so AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini cite a
[...]
inside generated responses. It matters because
[...]
37% of product discovery now
[...]
in AI interfaces, and the overlap between top Google links and AI-cited sources has dropped below 20%.
[...]
not optimized for AI answers risk becoming invisible at the moment of decision.
[...]
The conversation between Matt Britton and Todd Kaplan captured a turning point that most brand leaders have not yet operationalized. Discovery has moved from shelves and search results to AI-generated answers, and the gatekeeper is now an algorithm that decides which brands exist for a given query. The brands that win the next decade will be the ones that treat AEO with the rigor they once reserved for distribution and media, while still investing in the cultural tentpoles and human judgment that no model can replicate.
[...]
Britton's position, sharpened across more than 50 keynotes a year for Fortune 500 marketing and insights leaders, is that this is a window, not a permanent opening. Defaults are being set right now, inside millions of agent conversations, and they will be hard to unwind. For a deeper roadmap on building for AI-native consumers, Matt's national bestseller Generation AI provides the framework, and his keynotes give leadership teams the tactical playbook to compete for share of answer before competitors lock in the defaults.

results.title: The Kraft Heinz Company Automation Engineer Salaries in Chicago | Comparably
highlights:
The Kraft Heinz Company Automation Engineer Salaries in Chicago | Comparably

The Kraft Heinz Company Claimed Company

The Kraft Heinz Company (NASDAQ: KHC) is the third-largest food and beverage company in North America and the fifth-largest food and beverage company in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands. A globally trusted producer of delicious foods, The Kraft Heinz Company provides high quality, great taste and nutrition for all eating occasions whether at home, in restaurants or on the go. The Company’s iconic brands include Kraft, Heinz, ABC, Capri Sun, Classico, Jell-O, Kool-Aid, Lunchables, Maxwell House, Ore-Ida, Oscar Mayer, Philadelphia, Planters, Plasmon, Quero, Weight Watchers Smart Ones and Velveeta. The Kraft Heinz Company is dedicated to the sustainable health of our people, our planet and our Company. read more

# The Kraft Heinz Company Automation Engineer Salaries in Chicago

The average The Kraft Heinz Company Automation Engineer in Chicago earns an estimated $91,977 annually. The Kraft Heinz Company's Automation Engineer compensation is $838,304 less than the US average for a Automation Engineer.

In Chicago, The Engineering Department at The Kraft Heinz Company earns $9,733 more on average than the Product Department.

results.title: What's Your Biggest AI Ethical Nightmare? | Reid Blackman, CEO at Virtue Consultants | DataCamp
highlights:
Reid Blackman is the founder and CEO of Virtue, an AI ethical risk consultancy, and the author of The Ethical Nightmare Challenge: How to Avoid the Worst of AI (2026) and Ethical Machines (HBR Press, 2022). A former philosophy professor at Colgate with a PhD from the University of Texas at Austin, he has designed responsible AI programs for organizations including Amazon, Etsy, Kraft Heinz, Merck, US Bank, and Nationwide, and has advised the FBI, NASA, the World Economic Forum, and the Canadian government on federal AI regulations. He also hosts the Ethical Machines podcast.

results.title: Principal Data Scientist- Global Marketing Measurement and Optimization job at McCormick & Company in Hunt Valley, MD | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist- Global Marketing Measurement and Optimization job at McCormick & Company in Hunt Valley, MD | Wfhforgeon
[...]
# Principal Data Scientist- Global Marketing Measurement and Optimization job at McCormick & Company in Hunt Valley, MD
[...]
Title: Principal Data Scientist- Global Marketing Measurement & Optimization
[...]
We are looking to hire a Principal Data Scientist, Global Marketing Measurement and Optimization immediately in a Hybrid (50/50) capacity at our Global Headquarters in Hunt Valley, Maryland.
[...]
This Principal Data Scientist, Global Marketing Measurement and Optimization, will be responsible for bringing the data science expertise that is needed to lead a range of projects to drive better understanding of short- and long-term marketing effectiveness, including the next generation of technical modeling that powers McCormick's marketing measurement and optimization program. McCormick can drive unprecedented brand growth by learning how to better measure all aspects of marketing/media design and execution and bringing these different analytics together to provide a holistic view on the drivers of short- and long-term growth and build predictive models to enable better decision making. The role is designed to give McCormick the data and analytical engine to make this happen.
[...]
- Lead the design and implementation of next-gen media measurement and optimization capabilities, integrating marketing mix, long term brand equity measurement, in-flight measurement (e.g., lift studies, A/B testing), pre-market factors that affect media (e.g., creative testing)
- Develop media-driven volume forecasting tools to integrate into McCormick's demand forecasting platform
[...]
- Drive and leverage external connections to find new and improved tools and approaches to bring into McCormick
[...]
- Partner with vendors used for outsourced models, serving as lead consultant on technical methodology alignment integration with new capabilities
- Develop training content that enables business partners to use marketing optimization capabilities more effectively
[...]
### Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering or Data Analytics/Science, or closely related field
- 8+ years' experience in data science, with media analytics expertise
[...]
- Understanding of the business and technical aspects of Marketing Mix Modeling, Media Effectiveness Testing tools (Lift, A/B, etc.), Creative Testing, and proficiency for the evolution of and other tools in the Media Effectiveness Space
[...]
- Demonstrated skills and results in analyzing complex datasets, generating insights and building robust statistical models
- Demonstrated ability to connect the dots between complex data/analytics and business understanding
[...]
- Proficiency with building dashboards for business users using data visualization software (Power BI preferred)
[...]
- Expertise in SQL and Python coding
- Demonstrated experience with building data models, machine learning & AI
[...]
- Comfortable working with structured and unstructured data sources, e.g. syndicated data, SAP
[...]
- Demonstrated agile learning skills; ability to learn new technology, skills & tools quickly and easily

highlights:
# Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
JPMorganChase
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities
[...]
- Build and operate production generative artificial intelligence services and reusable backend application programming interfaces for firmwide use
[...]
- Combine enterprise data assets with large language and multimodal models to deliver high-quality user experiences
[...]
- Design scalable architectures with
[...]
- Collaborate with cloud engineering and site reliability engineering partners to deliver resilient
[...]
- Translate research
[...]
- Define and track measurable
[...]
- Ensure responsible artificial intelligence practices, controls, and governance are embedded into delivery and operations
[...]
- 3+ years of experience as an individual contributor in machine learning engineering or applied machine learning software engineering
[...]
- Demonstrated experience delivering production machine learning services in an enterprise environment, including being accountable for service health
[...]
- Strong fundamentals in statistics, optimization, and machine learning theory with applied depth in natural language processing and/or computer vision
[...]
- Hands-on experience building distributed, multi-threaded, and scalable systems (for example Ray, Horovod, or DeepSpeed)
[...]
- Experience designing and delivering service-oriented systems and application programming interfaces with scalability and performance requirements
[...]
- Strong problem-framing skills to align machine learning solutions to business objectives and constraints
[...]
- Experience designing and implementing pipeline workflows using directed acyclic graph frameworks (for example Kubeflow, DVC, or Ray)
[...]
- Experience building batch and streaming microservices exposed via gRPC and/or GraphQL
[...]
- Demonstrable experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning, quantization, and quantization-aware fine-tuning for large language models
[...]
- Experience with advanced prompting strategies such as chain-of-thought, tree-of-thought, or graph-of-thought approaches
[...]
- Experience with multimodal large language model use cases (text plus image, speech, or video)
[...]
- Experience partnering closely with cloud engineering and site reliability engineering teams on production readiness and operations
[...]
- Experience measuring and improving model quality using offline evaluation and production monitoring
[...]
